In Paris, an adult man was found unresponsive in the Canal Saint-Martin after reportedly swimming outside the designated area. As Europe's most severe heatwave on record continues, hospitals are admitting growing numbers of patients and emergency departments remain under intense pressure. According to Professor emeritus at the Catholic University of Louvain-la-Neuve, Jean-Pascal van Ypersele, unless global CO₂ emissions are significantly reduced, future heatwaves are likely to become even more extreme.

items.title: First Budapest Pride since Orbán ousted from office
items.description: "A new era has begun" is how some Hungarians are describing this year's Pride march, following 16 years of Viktor Orbán's government, during which the event was consistently restricted. France 24 correspondent Gulliver Cragg is in Budapest, where thousands are taking part in the parade despite scorching temperatures. The position of the newly elected Prime Minister, Péter Magyar, on LGBTQ+ rights remains ambiguous. He avoided the issue during the election campaign and is not attending today's Pride march. At the same time, he recently stated that "nobody should be stigmatised because of the way they love," leaving many uncertain about how his government will approach LGBTQ+ rights.

items.title: Right sweeps Latin America with new victory in Colombia
items.description: The right has won elections from Argentina to Honduras. But experts see little evidence of a structural ideological shift. Instead, they point to a political landscape that favours anti-establishment outsider candidates. Leer más

items.title: Bahrain accuses Iran of targeted drone attack after U.S. strikes Iran
items.description: A recent ceasefire agreement between Washington and Tehran appears even more fragile following the latest hostilities in the region, which includes U.S. strikes on Iranian military targets.

items.title: Magnitude 6.5 quake hits off southern Philippines
items.description: The 7.8-magnitude tremor that hit Mindanao on June 8 brought down buildings, triggered landslides and displaced thousands on the southern island, while also setting off tsunami warnings across the region

items.title: Hundreds arrested in Nairobi on anniversary of 2024 Gen Z protests
items.description: More than 300 people were arrested during protests in Nairobi on Thursday, police say, as Kenya marked two years since a landmark Gen Z uprising.

items.title: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany
items.description: A nine-year-old and five women were killed when Taleb Al-Abdulmohsen drove into the market in 2024.

items.title: UN Report accuses Israel of deliberately targeting children in Gaza
items.description: The report also details alleged abuses against Palestinian children in the occupied West Bank, including arbitrary detention, torture and sexual violence.

items.title: Lebanon: thousands mark Ashoura in Nabatieh amid rubble left by Israeli strikes
items.description: Thousands of Shiite Muslims marked Ashoura in Nabatieh on 26 June, holding processions through neighbourhoods devastated by recent Israeli airstrikes in a powerful display of resilience.

items.title: Europe's deadly heatwave breaks German record and halts public events
items.description: Germany's highest ever temperature of 41.3C is recorded provisionally in Saarbrücken, over the border from France.

items.title: Milei settles Argentina's US$15-million UN debt
items.description: Milei administration pays off Argentina’s outstanding US$15.6-million debt with the United Nations as campaigning for Rafael Grossi's secretary-general candidacy enters key stretch. Leer más

items.title: Iraq: Ashoura in Karbala draws thousands of Shiite pilgrims for rituals
items.description: Thousands of Shiite pilgrims mark Ashoura in Karbala, mourning Imam Hussein’s 680 AD death with processions, prayers and reinforced security.

items.title: South Africa inmates embrace art to curb repeat offending
items.description: At a correctional facility in Johannesburg, South Africa’s largest city, visitors are greeted by something unexpected: an art gallery. The exhibition of inmates' artwork is part of the country’s effort to reduce repeat offending through rehabilitation programs behind bars.

items.title: DR Congo takes Rwanda to international court over decades of conflict
items.description: Kinshasa accuses its neighbour of committing various violations since the 1994 Rwanda genocide.

items.title: South Africa vows firm response to anti-migrant violence
items.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa vowed on Thursday to crush any attempts to destabilise the nation during planned anti-immigrant marches next week, amid a wave of xenophobia

items.title: Sect leader convicted after girls found in trailer on US highway
items.description: A polygamous sect leader already serving a 50-year prison sentence in the US has been convicted of child abuse after girls were found in an unventilated trailer he was hauling through Arizona.

items.title: US, Lebanon, Israel sign framework agreement
items.description: US Secretary ‌of ⁠State ⁠Marco ​Rubio has announced ​a trilateral ​framework ‌agreement. Lebanese officials began direct talks with Israel in Washington in April.

items.title: Frozen feasts and cool pools help Rome zoo animals survive heatwave
items.description: As a severe heatwave grips Italy, Rome's Bioparco is helping its animals stay cool with frozen treats, chilled pools and climate-controlled shelters while the capital remains under a red heat alert.

items.title: German lawyers: Ban on far-right AfD 'likely successful'
items.description: A new report by legal experts has found that efforts to ban Germany's far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) could be successful — but there would be political dangers to such a move.

items.title: Hit South African show gets the world talking about polygamy and cheating
items.description: The Zulu-language series about a man with multiple wives is a worldwide hit.

items.title: Reports of Adorni's imminent departure from government ramp up
items.description: Rumours circulating that Cabinet Chief Manuel Adorni will leave the government imminently as further lurid headlines emerge in the press. Leer más

items.description: Mr Adelaja urged workers, particularly those who spend long hours in offices, to incorporate simple physical activities into their daily routines. The post Oakleaf partnership adds health focus to Lagos Public Service Club Games appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Scaloni expected to shuffle Argentina’s pack against Jordan

items.title: It's not just Cape Verde: African soccer is having a World Cup moment
items.description: The soccer greats — Brazil, England, Spain — have been giving uneven performances out on the pitch during this World Cup. Meanwhile, teams from Africa have been relentless.

items.title: Minimum wage has lost 40% of its purchasing power under Milei
items.description: Basic income for registered workers has plunged against November 2023 to levels even inferior to the 2001 crisis, according to a report by UBA Buenos Aires University’s Economics Faculty. Economic growth produces winners but they are not a majority. Leer más

items.title: With or without Messi? Argentina's big question against Jordan
items.description: Saturday's rotated side could still include players eager to maintain rhythm and those seeking it. Julián Álvarez is expected to start, with changes to the defence and midfield also likely. Leer más

items.title: Israel and Lebanon sign framework agreement after US-brokered talks
items.description: Hezbollah is not party to Friday's agreement, and previous ceasefires between Israel and Lebanon have still seen near-daily cross-border strikes.

items.title: Religion row as Texas makes Bible stories required reading in schools
items.description: Critics say the new reading requirements infringe on religious freedoms and blur the separation of church and state.

items.title: US sanctions Rwanda gold refinery accused of smuggling DR Congo's minerals
items.description: The US alleges Gasabo Gold Refinery smuggled at least 60kg of gold in early 2026.

items.title: Swatch seeks $170 million in damages from Samsung over trademark infringement
Did the French government prepare for this or not?
items.description: The heatwave that has gripped Europe for days is slowly shifting eastward, bringing some relief to France. But healthcare services continue to face intense pressure, prompting the Paris region to activate an emergency hospital plan. The French government continues to face criticism over its long-term response to climate change-driven heatwaves, with school closures and a lack of air conditioning in hospitals and retirement homes highlighting ongoing concerns. Peter O'Brien reports.

items.title: Moroccan court jails 29 in major drug trafficking case
items.description: A Moroccan court handed prison sentences to 29 people, including prominent politicians and sports and business figures, in an international drug trafficking and corruption scandal that has rocked the country.

items.title: Venezuela declares state of emergency after powerful twin earthquakes
items.description: Two massive earthquakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening; It remains unknown whether the incident caused fatalities, but some buildings collapsed and residents were driven into the streets in panic. Leer más

items.title: U.K. sets new June temperature record for third day in a row: Met Office
items.description: A provisional 37.3 C record temperature was chalked up in Santon Downham, a village in Suffolk, in southeast England, said the Met Office, breaking the previous high of 36.9°C set earlier

items.title: Venezuela earthquakes kill 920 people as international rescue teams arrive
items.description: Hundreds are still feared trapped under the rubble, as families desperate for news.

items.title: GTA 6 will launch as download only
items.description: Music and films are now largely digital-only - does the lack of a disc for GTA 6 mean gaming is going the same way?
items.source: does that mean the disc is dead?

items.title: Pope Leo XIV expected to visit Argentina in November on three-day stop
items.description: Argentine military bishop who met with pontiff at the Vatican this week says the itinerary is "practically defined" – though the Holy See has yet to issue an official announcement. Leer más

items.title: Solidarity rally in Ohio after court ends protected status for Haitians
items.description: Residents gathered Thursday in Ohio in support of Haitian migrants following a Supreme Court decision to allow the Trump administration to end legal protections for migrants from Haiti and Syria fleeing violence and natural disaster in their country

items.title: Milei shuns Wall Street to raise dollars at rate lower than 7%
items.description: Luis Caputo stuck to a strategy that many investors initially saw as too risky: secure dollars through cheaper alternative channels, buy time and wait for spreads to come down. Leer más

items.title: Ebola cases jump as Congo tightens travel rules
items.description: Health officials said increased epidemiological surviellance was helping to detect cases earlier. They also said that community spread of the virus had grown

items.title: Watch: BBC reports from La Guaira, one of Venezuela's worst-hit areas
items.description: Vanessa Silva visited the coastal state, where rescuers are still working to find survivors under the rubble after twin earthquakes rocked the country.

items.title: Young people avoiding the news, Reuters report
items.description: An alarming number of young people are turning away from the news, according to the Reuters Digital News Report presented at DW's Global Media Forum. Despite this, the report's author says, "journalism still matters."

items.title: Kenya Police sued over Nairobi lockdown during Gen Z protest anniversary
items.description: Kenya's police are facing legal action after shutting down central Nairobi during protests marking the second anniversary of the Gen Z uprising.

items.title: Fact check: No, Zelenskyy wasn't killed in a Russian airstrike
items.description: Amid a flare-up in violence between Russia and Ukraine, viral posts claim Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has died. DW Fact check takes a closer look.

items.title: Just how much trouble is Canada's economy in?
items.description: Five charts help illustrate the state of Canada's economy - and how it's performing compared to other wealthy nations.

items.title: Utah declares state of emergency, restricts fireworks as U.S. largest wildfire grows
items.description: The National Weather Service issued a rare “Particularly Dangerous Situation” warning as dry, windy conditions provided fuel for more fires across the western U.S.

items.title: Historic joy in Abidjan as Ivory Coast reach World Cup knockouts for first time
items.description: Ivory Coast reaches World Cup knockout stage for the first time after Nicolas Pépé’s brace beats Curaçao 2-0, sparking huge celebrations in Abidjan.

items.title: The 'Flamingo Revolution': What's behind Albania's protests?
items.description: For weeks now, images of crowds of Albanians protesting on the streets have been relayed around the world. The protesters say they are fighting for democracy. PM Edi Rama insists the movement is part of a hybrid war.
